window_summary: This window tracks the recent release evolution of shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android from v5.0.0 through v5.3.5-nightly, covering feature additions like subscription support, AEAD-2022 protocol, Android version compatibility updates, and numerous bug fixes.

chunks.title: v5.0.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.0.0
Tag: v5.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2020-01-14T02:48:42Z; created_at: 2020-01-14T02:05:47Z
Release notes:
* (mobile) Add subscription support. (#2392, #2402)
* Support IPv6 for ACL rules. (#2386)
* Minor bug fixes and improvements. (#2334, #2335, #2348, #2382)
Plugin library has been updated to 1.3.3 (#2391, #2398).
For documentation of the subscription file, see [here](https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/blob/master/.github/doc-json.md).
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.0.0, the project adds subscription support for mobile, IPv6 support for ACL rules, and includes minor bug fixes and improvements. The plugin library is updated to 1.3.3.

chunks.title: v5.1.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.1.0
Tag: v5.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2020-05-04T08:58:13Z; created_at: 2020-05-04T08:51:31Z
Release notes:
* Add support for `plain` method. (#1046, #2454)
* Hardware acceleration for AES. (#2212, #2454, #2503)
* Refined ACL support. (#2452, #2454, #2508)
* TCP fast open and hosts are deprecated for now. (#2454, #2501, #2502)
* Minor bug fixes. (#2439, #2490, #2506)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.1.0, support for the 'plain' method is added, hardware acceleration for AES is introduced, ACL support is refined, TCP fast open and hosts are deprecated, and minor bug fixes are included.

chunks.title: v5.1.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.1.1
Tag: v5.1.1; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2020-05-21T00:52:04Z; created_at: 2020-05-19T01:29:06Z
Release notes:
* Dummy cipher's name is changed to `none`. (https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-org/issues/161)
* Local DNS also running over TCP. (https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/pull/246)
* Minor bug fixes. (#2517, #2526, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/issues/234, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/issues/237, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/pull/239, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/issues/240, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/pull/241, https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-rust/pull/242)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.1.1, the dummy cipher's name is changed to 'none', local DNS now runs over TCP, and minor bug fixes are applied.

chunks.title: v5.1.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.1.2
Tag: v5.1.2; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2020-07-08T03:02:38Z; created_at: 2020-07-08T02:56:53Z
Release notes:
* Support for Android 11. (#2507, #2548)
* (mobile) Built-in QR code scanner now uses machine learning technology and no longer requires Google play services. (#2548, #2551)
* Minor bug fixes. (#2546)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.1.2, support for Android 11 is added, the built-in QR code scanner is updated to use machine learning technology and no longer requires Google Play services, and minor bug fixes are included.

chunks.title: v5.3.4
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.3.4
Tag: v5.3.4; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-10-07T02:13:51Z; created_at: 2024-08-29T16:08:37Z
Release notes:
Multiple fixes for Android 14 (thanks to @AaronChen0).
## What's Changed
* Hotfix for password checking fail by @dev4u in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3027
* Corrected range for move operation by @MSVhora in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3035
* Add githubusercontent.com to gfwlist by @AaronChen0 in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3039
* Fix base64 check by @AaronChen0 in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3045
* Update shadowsocks-rust by @zedifen in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3119
* fix implicit-function-declaration error when updating NDK to r26+ by @AaronChen0 in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3163
* Target Android 14 by @AaronChen0 in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3165
* Fix a warning from sslocal logcat output by @AaronChen0 in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3166
## New Contributors
* @MSVhora made their first contribution in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3035
* @AaronChen0 made their first contribution in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3039
* @zedifen made their first contribution in https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/pull/3119
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/compare/v5.3.3...v5.3.4
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.3.4, multiple fixes for Android 14 are applied, including a hotfix for password checking, a corrected range for move operations, addition of githubusercontent.com to gfwlist, a base64 check fix, an update to shadowsocks-rust, a fix for an implicit-function-declaration error when updating NDK to r26+, targeting Android 14, and a fix for a warning from sslocal logcat output.

chunks.title: v5.3.5-nightly
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android
Release: v5.3.5-nightly
Tag: v5.3.5-nightly; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-02-10T06:47:17Z; created_at: 2026-02-10T06:43:48Z
Release notes:
## What's Changed
### Bug Fixes
- Fix DNS resolution failure on some devices (OnePlus 12 / Android 15) (#3185)
- Fix profileName fallback missing
- Fix desugaring issue
- Fix build staleness
- Ensure VpnService is prepared before connecting
### Improvements
- VPN flags are now opt-in for plugins (#3227)
- Eliminate gson dependency
- Remove non-existent include dirs (#3198)
- Refine code style
- Add E2E test GitHub Actions workflow with x86_64 emulator
### Dependencies
- Update all dependencies to latest versions
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android/compare/v5.3.4...v5.3.5-nightly
---
*This release was created automatically by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code).*
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release v5.3.5-nightly, bug fixes include a DNS resolution failure on some devices (OnePlus 12 / Android 15), a missing profileName fallback, a desugaring issue, build staleness, and ensuring VpnService is prepared before connecting. Improvements include making VPN flags opt-in for plugins, eliminating the gson dependency, removing non-existent include dirs, refining code style, and adding an E2E test GitHub Actions workflow with an x86_64 emulator. All dependencies are updated to their latest versions.

questions.query_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android releases, which release adds subscription support for mobile and IPv6 support for ACL rules?

answer_support.answer_text: adds subscription support for mobile, IPv6 support for ACL rules

questions.query_text: Across the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android releases, how does the cipher-related change in v5.1.9 differ from the cipher-related change in v5.2.3?

options.text: v5.1.9 removes obsolete ciphers, while v5.2.3 adds back the xchacha20-ietf-poly1305 cipher.
v5.1.9 adds the xchacha20-ietf-poly1305 cipher, while v5.2.3 removes obsolete ciphers.
Both releases only update the plugin library without cipher changes.
v5.1.9 deprecates the 'plain' method, while v5.2.3 introduces AEAD-2022.

questions.expected_answer: v5.1.9 removes obsolete ciphers, while v5.2.3 adds back the xchacha20-ietf-poly1305 cipher.

questions.query_text: In the shadowsocks/shadowsocks-android release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for Android 11 and updates the QR code scanner to use machine learning, (2) a release that adds support for Android 12, (3) a release that adds support for Android 13, (4) a release that applies multiple fixes for Android 14.

answer_support.answer_text: support for Android 11 is added, the built-in QR code scanner is updated to use machine learning technology
